// ============================================
// Photography Pixel Hub ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Prisma Schema
// ============================================

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
}

// ============================================
// Enums
// ============================================

enum UserRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  EDITOR
  MEDIA_BUYER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  CLIENT
}

enum ProjectStatus {
  DRAFT
  PUBLISHED
  ARCHIVED
  EXPIRED
}

enum VideoStatus {
  PENDING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  FAILED
}

enum ActivityType {
  CREATE
  UPDATE
  DELETE
  LOGIN
  LOGOUT
  UPLOAD
  DOWNLOAD
  VIEW
  ARCHIVE
  RESTORE
}

enum ClientStatus {
  ACTIVE
  ARCHIVED
  BLOCKED
}

// ============================================
// Models
// ============================================

model User {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  email     String   @unique
  name      String
  password  String
  role      UserRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  avatar    String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  clients      Client[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]

  @@index([email])
  @@map("users")
}

model Client {
  id         String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId     String       @db.Uuid
  clientCode String       @unique
  name       String
  company    String?
  email      String
  phone      String?
  notes      String?
  status     ClientStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  token      String       @unique
  archivedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime      @updatedAt

  user      User       @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projects  Project[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@unique([userId, email])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([email])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([clientCode])
  @@map("clients")
}

model Project {
  id          String         @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  clientId    String         @db.Uuid
  name        String
  description String?
  status      ProjectStatus  @default(DRAFT)
  token       String         @unique
  expiresAt   DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime       @updatedAt

  client    Client    @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  videos    Video[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([token])
  @@map("projects")
}

model Video {
  id            String      @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId     String      @db.Uuid
  title         String
  key           String   @unique
  size          BigInt
  duration      Int?
  status        VideoStatus @default(PENDING)
  thumbnailKey  String?
  createdAt     DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime    @updatedAt

  project   Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@map("videos")
}

model Download {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid

// ============================================
// Photography Pixel Hub ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Prisma Schema
// ============================================

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
}

// ============================================
// Enums
// ============================================

enum UserRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  EDITOR
  MEDIA_BUYER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  CLIENT
}

enum ProjectStatus {
  DRAFT
  PUBLISHED
  ARCHIVED
  EXPIRED
}

enum VideoStatus {
  PENDING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  FAILED
}

enum ActivityType {
  CREATE
  UPDATE
  DELETE
  LOGIN
  LOGOUT
  UPLOAD
  DOWNLOAD
  VIEW
  ARCHIVE
  RESTORE
}

enum ClientStatus {
  ACTIVE
  ARCHIVED
  BLOCKED
}

// ============================================
// Models
// ============================================

model User {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  email     String   @unique
  name      String
  password  String
  role      UserRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  avatar    String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  clients      Client[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]

  @@index([email])
  @@map("users")
}

model Client {
  id         String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId     String       @db.Uuid
  clientCode String       @unique
  name       String
  company    String?
  email      String
  phone      String?
  notes      String?
  status     ClientStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  token      String       @unique
  archivedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime      @updatedAt

  user      User       @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projects  Project[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@unique([userId, email])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([email])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([clientCode])
  @@map("clients")
}

model Project {
  id          String         @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  clientId    String         @db.Uuid
  name        String
  description String?
  status      ProjectStatus  @default(DRAFT)
  token       String         @unique
  expiresAt   DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime       @updatedAt

  client    Client    @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  videos    Video[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([token])
  @@map("projects")
}

model Video {
  id            String      @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId     String      @db.Uuid
  title         String
  key           String   @unique
  size          BigInt
  duration      Int?
  status        VideoStatus @default(PENDING)
  thumbnailKey  String?
  createdAt     DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime    @updatedAt

  project   Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@map("videos")
}

model Download {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  video   Video   @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  client  Client  @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("downloads")
}

model View {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("views")
}

model ActivityLog {
  id        String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid

Functions: `create()`, `update()`, `archive()` (soft delete), `restore()`, `list()`, `count()`, `getStatistics()`, `exportCsv()` - Normalizes input: trims strings, lowercases email, converts empty strings to `null` - Generates unique sequential client codes (`CL-000001`) via transactional code gen - Generates tokens via `generateToken(32)` from utils - Logs activity via `activityService.log()` when `actorId` is provided - `archive()` is soft delete (sets `status=ARCHIVED, archivedAt=now()`), never hard deletes - `restore()` sets `status=ACTIVE, archivedAt=null` - Also has `user.service.ts`, `auth.service.ts`, `project.service.ts`, `video.service.ts`, `download.service.ts`, `activity.service.ts` **Layer 3: Repositories** (`src/repositories/client.repository.ts`) - **The ONLY layer that touches Prisma** вҖ” has `import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"` - Exports `clientRepository` object with methods: `findById`, `findByClientCode`, `findByToken`, `findByEmail`, `create`, `update`, `softDelete`, `restore`, `delete` (hard delete, test-only), `findMany` (paginated + search/filter/sort), `count`, `getStatistics`, `findAllForExport` - `buildWhere()` helper constructs WHERE clause with search OR conditions across 5 fields (clientCode, name, company, phone, email) - `buildOrderBy()` maps sort strings to Prisma orderBy objects - Always includes relations via `include` in find methods - Repository types derived from Prisma: `ClientWithRelations`, `ClientCreateInput`, etc. **Prisma Client** (`src/lib/prisma/client.ts`): ```ts import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client"; import { PrismaPg } from "@prisma/adapter-pg"; // Uses Prisma Accelerate PG adapter, singleton pattern // Exported as named export `prisma` ``` --- ### 4. Types are extracted with `z.infer`: ```ts export type CreateClientInput = z.infer; export type ClientFilterValue = z.infer; ``` --- ### 5. UI Components **UI Library:** shadcn/ui with New York style, zinc base color, CSS variables, RSC mode, Tailwind v4 **Existing shadcn/ui primitives** (`src/components/ui/`): `avatar`, `badge`, `button`, `card`, `dialog`, `dropdown-menu`, `input`, `label`, `separator`, `sheet`, `skeleton`, `tooltip` **Dialog/Modal:** - `src/components/ui/dialog.tsx` вҖ” Full Radix-based Dialog with overlay, animations (data-state based), RTL-aware positioning (`start-1/2`, `translate-x-[-50%]`) - `src/components/shared/confirm-dialog.tsx` вҖ” Reusable confirmation wrapper with title, description, confirm/cancel labels (default Arabic), and destructive/default variants **Table:** - `src/components/shared/data-table.tsx` вҖ” **Empty file** (stub, not yet implemented) - `src/components/clients/client-table.tsx` вҖ” **Custom table implementation** with: - Search input with debounce (300ms) - Filter tabs (all/active/archived/blocked) - Sort dropdown (newest/oldest/alphabetical) - Pagination (server-side with page/pageSize controls) - Loading skeleton state - Empty state with `EmptyState` component - Row dropdown menu (view details, edit, archive/restore) - Uses `useTransition` for loading state, `useCallback`/`useMemo` for optimization - Responsive column visibility (hidden on different breakpoints) - Direct Prisma action calls from client component **Form pattern** (`src/components/forms/client-form.tsx`): - Uses `react-hook-form` with `@hookform/resolvers/zod` (`zodResolver`) - Modal-based (rendered inside Dialog from shadcn/ui) - `useForm` with `register()`, `handleSubmit()`, `reset()`, `formState.errors` - Populates form via `reset()` in `useEffect` for edit mode - Calls server actions directly (passing FormData) - Error display via `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby`, and `

` elements - Labels with required field indicators (`*`) - LTR overrides for email/phone fields (`dir="ltr"`) --- ### 6. Testing Setup **Config** (`vitest.config.ts`): ```ts import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config"; export default defineConfig({ resolve: { alias: { "@": resolve(__dirname, "src") } }, test: { globals: true, environment: "node", include: ["src/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts"], }, }); ``` **Test files found:** - `src/repositories/__tests__/client.repository.test.ts` (15 tests) - `src/services/__tests__/client.service.test.ts` (12 tests) - `src/services/__tests__/auth.service.test.ts` - `src/lib/validations/__tests__/client.test.ts` (15 tests) - `src/lib/validations/__tests__/auth.test.ts` - `src/lib/auth/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` - `src/lib/auth/__tests__/session.test.ts` **Mocking pattern:** ```ts const { mockClient: mockClientModel } = vi.hoisted(() => ({ mockClient: { findUnique: vi.fn(), findFirst: vi.fn(), findMany: vi.fn(), create: vi.fn(), update: vi.fn(), delete: vi.fn(), count: vi.fn(), }, })); vi.mock("@/lib/prisma", () => ({ prisma: { client: mockClientModel, ... }, })); ``` Tests follow: `describe` вҶ’ `it` вҶ’ `expect`, with `beforeEach(() => vi.clearAllMocks())`. Repository tests mock `prisma` directly. Service tests mock `clientRepository` and `activityService`. Validation tests use `safeParse()` and assert on `.success` and `.error.issues[0]?.message`. --- ### 7. package.json Dependencies **Core:** Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 6, Prisma 7.8 (with `@prisma/adapter-pg`) **Auth:** `jose` (JWT), `bcryptjs`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js` **UI:** `@radix-ui/*` (avatar, dialog, dropdown-menu, label, slot, tooltip), `lucide-react`, `class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `next-themes`, `sonner` (toasts) **Forms:** `react-hook-form`, `@hookform/resolvers`, `zod` v4 **Data:** `@tanstack/react-query`, `@aws-sdk/client-s3`, `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner` **Email:** `@react-email/components` **Dev:** `vitest` v4, `eslint` v9, `postcss`, `tailwindcss` v4, `tw-animate-css`, `tsx` --- ### 8. components.json (shadcn/ui config) ```json { "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "tailwind.config.ts", "css": "src/app/globals.css", "baseColor": "zinc", "cssVariables": true }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils/cn", "ui": "@/components/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` --- ### 9. lib/utils **`cn.ts`** вҖ” `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` wrapper for class name merging. **`format.ts`** вҖ” `formatBytes()`, `formatDate()` (Arabic locale `ar-SA`, `Intl.DateTimeFormat`), `formatDateTime()`, `formatNumber()` (Arabic locale number formatting). **`index.ts`** вҖ” Re-exports cn + format, plus `generateToken(length=32)` (crypto random, alphanumeric), `sleep(ms)`. --- ### 10. Type Patterns **Types directory** (`src/types/`): - `index.ts` вҖ” barrel export for all types - `auth.ts` вҖ” `UserRole` (from Prisma), `AuthenticatedUser`, `SessionUser` (nullable), `SessionStatus` - `client.ts` вҖ” `ClientWithProjects`, `ClientTableRow`, `PaginatedClients`, `ClientStatistics`, `ClientTimelineEntry`, `ClientDetails` - `project.ts` вҖ” `ProjectWithVideos`, `ProjectSummary`, `PaginatedProjects` - `analytics.ts`, `upload.ts` вҖ” additional domain types **Pattern:** Types extend Prisma types using `Prisma.xxxGetPayload<>` for include-based types, and custom interfaces/types for app-level DTOs. Status enums come from Prisma and are re-exported through types. --- ### Key Observations for Building New Modules 1. **Follow the exact 3-layer pattern:** Action вҶ’ Service вҶ’ Repository (never skip layers) 2. **All Arabic error messages** in Zod schemas and action returns 3. **Soft delete is the standard** вҖ” never hard delete in production; use ARCHIVED status + `archivedAt` timestamp 4. **Activity logging** via `activityService.log()` with `actorId` pattern 5. **Auth pattern:** `getCurrentUser()` returns `null` (non-throwing), check and return error state 6. **Form actions** use `(_prev: StateType, formData: FormData)` signature with `useActionState` 7. **The `data-table.tsx` is a stub** вҖ” the existing client table is custom-built; a new module should either build its own custom table or implement the shared data-table 8. **Client Table is the most complete reference** вҖ” it demonstrates search, filter, sort, pagination, CSV export, optimistic UI, and modal-based CRUD

Returns typed state objects: `{ success: boolean; error?: string; ... }` - Uses `getCurrentUser()` from `@/lib/auth/session` for authentication вҖ” returns `null` if not logged in (non-throwing) - Form actions receive `(_prev: StateType, formData: FormData)` signature - Error messages are all in Arabic - Example: `createClientAction`, `updateClientAction`, `deleteClientAction`, `getClientsAction`, etc. **Layer 2: Services** (`src/services/client.service.ts`) - Business logic layer вҖ” calls repositories only, never Prisma directly - Functions: `create()`, `update()`, `archive()` (soft delete), `restore()`, `list()`, `count()`, `getStatistics()`, `exportCsv()` - Normalizes input: trims strings, lowercases email, converts empty strings to `null` - Generates unique sequential client codes (`CL-000001`) via transactional code gen - Generates tokens via `generateToken(32)` from utils - Logs activity via `activityService.log()` when `actorId` is provided - `archive()` is soft delete (sets `status=ARCHIVED, archivedAt=now()`), never hard deletes - `restore()` sets `status=ACTIVE, archivedAt=null` - Also has `user.service.ts`, `auth.service.ts`, `project.service.ts`, `video.service.ts`, `download.service.ts`, `activity.service.ts` **Layer 3: Repositories** (`src/repositories/client.repository.ts`) - **The ONLY layer that touches Prisma** вҖ” has `import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"` - Exports `clientRepository` object with methods: `findById`, `findByClientCode`, `findByToken`, `findByEmail`, `create`, `update`, `softDelete`, `restore`, `delete` (hard delete, test-only), `findMany` (paginated + search/filter/sort), `count`, `getStatistics`, `findAllForExport` - `buildWhere()` helper constructs WHERE clause with search OR conditions across 5 fields (clientCode, name, company, phone, email) - `buildOrderBy()` maps sort strings to Prisma orderBy objects - Always includes relations via `include` in find methods - Repository types derived from Prisma: `ClientWithRelations`, `ClientCreateInput`, etc. **Prisma Client** (`src/lib/prisma/client.ts`): ```ts import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client"; import { PrismaPg } from "@prisma/adapter-pg"; // Uses Prisma Accelerate PG adapter, singleton pattern // Exported as named export `prisma` ``` --- ### 4. Validation Patterns (Zod + Arabic Messages) All schemas are in `src/lib/validations/`: - **`auth.ts`** вҖ” Reusable primitives: `emailSchema`, `passwordSchema` (min 8, uppercase, lowercase, digit), `nameSchema` (2вҖ“50 chars), `phoneSchema` (Saudi regex `05xxxxxxxx`) - **`client.ts`** вҖ” `clientStatusSchema`, `clientFilterSchema`, `clientSortSchema`, `createClientSchema`, `updateClientSchema`, `clientQuerySchema` - **`project.ts`** вҖ” `projectStatusSchema`, `createProjectSchema`, `updateProjectSchema`, `projectQuerySchema` - **`upload.ts`**, **`download.ts`**, **`video.ts`** вҖ” Domain-specific schemas **Pattern for Arabic messages:** ```ts export const emailSchema = z.string() .min(1, "Ш§Щ„ШЁШұЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩ„ЩғШӘШұЩҲЩҶЩҠ Щ…Ш·Щ„ЩҲШЁ") .email("Ш§Щ„ШЁШұЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩ„ЩғШӘШұЩҲЩҶЩҠ ШәЩҠШұ ШөШ§Щ„Шӯ"); export const nameSchema = z.string() .min(2, "Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіЩ… ЩҠШ¬ШЁ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩғЩҲЩҶ ШӯШұЩҒЩҠЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ШЈЩӮЩ„") .max(50, "Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіЩ… ЩҠШ¬ШЁ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩғЩҲЩҶ 50 ШӯШұЩҒШ§ЩӢ ЩғШӯШҜ ШЈЩӮШөЩү"); export const phoneSchema = z.string() .regex(/^(\+?966|0)?5\d{8}$/, "ШұЩӮЩ… Ш§Щ„ЩҮШ§ШӘЩҒ ШәЩҠШұ ШөШ§Щ„Шӯ вҖ” Щ…Ш«Ш§Щ„: 05xxxxxxxx") .optional().or(z.literal("")); ``` All validation error messages are in Arabic. `z.literal("")` is used alongside `.optional()` to allow empty strings for optional fields (needed because FormData sends empty strings for empty inputs). ` elements - Labels with required field indicators (`*`) - LTR overrides for email/phone fields (`dir="ltr"`) --- ### 6. Testing Setup **Config** (`vitest.config.ts`): ```ts import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config"; export default defineConfig({ resolve: { alias: { "@": resolve(__dirname, "src") } }, test: { globals: true, environment: "node", include: ["src/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts"], }, }); ``` **Test files found:** - `src/repositories/__tests__/client.repository.test.ts` (15 tests) - `src/services/__tests__/client.service.test.ts` (12 tests) - `src/services/__tests__/auth.service.test.ts` - `src/lib/validations/__tests__/client.test.ts` (15 tests) - `src/lib/validations/__tests__/auth.test.ts` - `src/lib/auth/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` - `src/lib/auth/__tests__/session.test.ts` **Mocking pattern:** ```ts const { mockClient: mockClientModel } = vi.hoisted(() => ({ mockClient: { findUnique: vi.fn(), findFirst: vi.fn(), findMany: vi.fn(), create: vi.fn(), update: vi.fn(), delete: vi.fn(), count: vi.fn(), }, })); vi.mock("@/lib/prisma", () => ({ prisma: { client: mockClientModel, ... }, })); ``` Tests follow: `describe` вҶ’ `it` вҶ’ `expect`, with `beforeEach(() => vi.clearAllMocks())`. Repository tests mock `prisma` directly. Service tests mock `clientRepository` and `activityService`. Validation tests use `safeParse()` and assert on `.success` and `.error.issues[0]?.message`. --- ### 7. package.json Dependencies **Core:** Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 6, Prisma 7.8 (with `@prisma/adapter-pg`) **Auth:** `jose` (JWT), `bcryptjs`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js` **UI:** `@radix-ui/*` (avatar, dialog, dropdown-menu, label, slot, tooltip), `lucide-react`, `class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `next-themes`, `sonner` (toasts) **Forms:** `react-hook-form`, `@hookform/resolvers`, `zod` v4 **Data:** `@tanstack/react-query`, `@aws-sdk/client-s3`, `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner` **Email:** `@react-email/components` **Dev:** `vitest` v4, `eslint` v9, `postcss`, `tailwindcss` v4, `tw-animate-css`, `tsx` --- ### 8. components.json (shadcn/ui config) ```json { "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "tailwind.config.ts", "css": "src/app/globals.css", "baseColor": "zinc", "cssVariables": true }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils/cn", "ui": "@/components/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` --- ### 9. lib/utils **`cn.ts`** вҖ” `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` wrapper for class name merging. **`format.ts`** вҖ” `formatBytes()`, `formatDate()` (Arabic locale `ar-SA`, `Intl.DateTimeFormat`), `formatDateTime()`, `formatNumber()` (Arabic locale number formatting). **`index.ts`** вҖ” Re-exports cn + format, plus `generateToken(length=32)` (crypto random, alphanumeric), `sleep(ms)`. --- ### 10. Type Patterns **Types directory** (`src/types/`): - `index.ts` вҖ” barrel export for all types - `auth.ts` вҖ” `UserRole` (from Prisma), `AuthenticatedUser`, `SessionUser` (nullable), `SessionStatus` - `client.ts` вҖ” `ClientWithProjects`, `ClientTableRow`, `PaginatedClients`, `ClientStatistics`, `ClientTimelineEntry`, `ClientDetails` - `project.ts` вҖ” `ProjectWithVideos`, `ProjectSummary`, `PaginatedProjects` - `analytics.ts`, `upload.ts` вҖ” additional domain types **Pattern:** Types extend Prisma types using `Prisma.xxxGetPayload<>` for include-based types, and custom interfaces/types for app-level DTOs. Status enums come from Prisma and are re-exported through types. --- ### Key Observations for Building New Modules 1. **Follow the exact 3-layer pattern:** Action вҶ’ Service вҶ’ Repository (never skip layers) 2. **All Arabic error messages** in Zod schemas and action returns 3. **Soft delete is the standard** вҖ” never hard delete in production; use ARCHIVED status + `archivedAt` timestamp 4. **Activity logging** via `activityService.log()` with `actorId` pattern 5. **Auth pattern:** `getCurrentUser()` returns `null` (non-throwing), check and return error state 6. **Form actions** use `(_prev: StateType, formData: FormData)` signature with `useActionState` 7. **The `data-table.tsx` is a stub** вҖ” the existing client table is custom-built; a new module should either build its own custom table or implement the shared data-table 8. **Client Table is the most complete reference** вҖ” it demonstrates search, filter, sort, pagination, CSV export, optimistic UI, and modal-based CRUD Next steps: none

Validation tests use `safeParse()` and assert on `.success` and `.error.issues[0]?.message`. --- ### 7. package.json Dependencies **Core:** Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 6, Prisma 7.8 (with `@prisma/adapter-pg`) **Auth:** `jose` (JWT), `bcryptjs`, `@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js` **UI:** `@radix-ui/*` (avatar, dialog, dropdown-menu, label, slot, tooltip), `lucide-react`, `class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `next-themes`, `sonner` (toasts) **Forms:** `react-hook-form`, `@hookform/resolvers`, `zod` v4 **Data:** `@tanstack/react-query`, `@aws-sdk/client-s3`, `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner` **Email:** `@react-email/components` **Dev:** `vitest` v4, `eslint` v9, `postcss`, `tailwindcss` v4, `tw-animate-css`, `tsx` --- ### 8. components.json (shadcn/ui config) ```json { "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "tailwind.config.ts", "css": "src/app/globals.css", "baseColor": "zinc", "cssVariables": true }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils/cn", "ui": "@/components/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` --- ### 9. lib/utils **`cn.ts`** вҖ” `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` wrapper for class name merging. **`format.ts`** вҖ” `formatBytes()`, `formatDate()` (Arabic locale `ar-SA`, `Intl.DateTimeFormat`), `formatDateTime()`, `formatNumber()` (Arabic locale number formatting). **`index.ts`** вҖ” Re-exports cn + format, plus `generateToken(length=32)` (crypto random, alphanumeric), `sleep(ms)`. --- ### 10. Type Patterns **Types directory** (`src/types/`): - `index.ts` вҖ” barrel export for all types - `auth.ts` вҖ” `UserRole` (from Prisma), `AuthenticatedUser`, `SessionUser` (nullable), `SessionStatus` - `client.ts` вҖ” `ClientWithProjects`, `ClientTableRow`, `PaginatedClients`, `ClientStatistics`, `ClientTimelineEntry`, `ClientDetails` - `project.ts` вҖ” `ProjectWithVideos`, `ProjectSummary`, `PaginatedProjects` - `analytics.ts`, `upload.ts` вҖ” additional domain types **Pattern:** Types extend Prisma types using `Prisma.xxxGetPayload<>` for include-based types, and custom interfaces/types for app-level DTOs. Status enums come from Prisma and are re-exported through types. --- ### Key Observations for Building New Modules 1. **Follow the exact 3-layer pattern:** Action вҶ’ Service вҶ’ Repository (never skip layers) 2. **All Arabic error messages** in Zod schemas and action returns 3. **Soft delete is the standard** вҖ” never hard delete in production; use ARCHIVED status + `archivedAt` timestamp 4. **Activity logging** via `activityService.log()` with `actorId` pattern 5. **Auth pattern:** `getCurrentUser()` returns `null` (non-throwing), check and return error state 6. **Form actions** use `(_prev: StateType, formData: FormData)` signature with `useActionState` 7. **The `data-table.tsx` is a stub** вҖ” the existing client table is custom-built; a new module should either build its own custom table or implement the shared data-table 8. **Client Table is the most complete reference** вҖ” it demonstrates search, filter, sort, pagination, CSV export, optimistic UI, and modal-based CRUD Next steps: none

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once вҖ” it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model вҖ” use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 вҖ” pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only вҖ” combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly вҖ” large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
